items.description: The representation of an employee's Section 603 high earner status for a specific year. Section 603 of the SECURE 2.0 Act requires employees aged 50 or older whose prior-year FICA wages exceed the IRS threshold to have their catch-up contributions to pre-tax retirement benefits designated as post-tax contributions.

id.description: The unique identifier of the Section 603 high earner status record

effective_year.description: The year for which this high earner status applies

is_high_earner.description: Whether the employee is classified as a high earner for Section 603 purposes. Can be null if the status has not yet been determined.

items.title: Employee-Section603-High-Earner-Status

schema.description:
Not Found 
  
The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided ID/UUID is valid.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

errors.message: The requested resource was not found.

schema.title: Not-Found-Error-Object
